summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates wallet/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine system enumeration (os.hostname(), execSync, whoami, ping), filesystem traversal (fs.existsSync over user paths), wallet/credential scanning, and outbound HTTPS POST/GET to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (scanner.js:161, scanner.js:323). The wallet-scanner.js module further confirms wallet-targeted reconnaissance (curl, ping, POST loops at L4/L723/L795/L871). The combination of crypto-wallet enumeration, host/system fingerprinting, and a hardcoded GitHub Pages-hosted C2 used as both fetch and POST destination is the unambiguous shape of an installer-side credential/wallet stealer with attacker benefit (exfiltration of installer secrets and wallet data). The package's stated purpose ('DeFi env auditor') is a cover story consistent with the wallet-scanner module's targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
